title: Waiting at the dentist in Vilnius
summary: A short story about a long wait at a clinic abroad and what you noticed in the room.
topic: Waiting at the dentist in Vilnius
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a long wait at a clinic, doctor's office, or pharmacy abroad. Describe what you saw and heard while you waited. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: Last winter in Vilnius, I had a small problem with one tooth, so…
The waiting room was small and warm, with…
While I was waiting, a little girl on the bench opposite me…
sampleText: Last winter in Vilnius, I had a small problem with one tooth, so a colleague booked an appointment for me at a dental clinic on Pylimo street. I arrived twenty minutes early because I was nervous and could not sit still in my flat. The waiting room was small and warm, with three wooden chairs, a low table covered in old magazines I could not read, and a large round clock above the door. While I was waiting, a little girl on the bench opposite me kept watching me out of the corner of her eye. Her mother was reading a newspaper and pretending not to notice. After about ten minutes, the girl walked across the room, put a small green sweet on my knee, and ran back to her mother. I tried to thank her in Lithuanian, but I only knew two words. The receptionist looked up from her computer, smiled, and translated for me in soft English. When the dentist finally called my name, the wait had felt much shorter than I expected. I left with a clean filling, a green sweet wrapper in my pocket, and a small new respect for the patience of children in waiting rooms.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous for parallel ongoing actions
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'While I was waiting, a little girl... kept watching me' và 'Her mother was reading a newspaper' — past continuous tả nhiều hành động đang xảy ra song song trong cùng một khung cảnh.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'While I was waiting, a little girl... kept watching me' and 'Her mother was reading a newspaper' — past continuous depicts multiple ongoing actions in the same scene.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for retrospective evaluation
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'the wait had felt much shorter than I expected' dùng past perfect ('had felt') để nhìn lại cuộc chờ từ thời điểm sau đó.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'The wait had felt much shorter than I expected' uses past perfect ('had felt') to look back on the wait from a later moment.

sampleTranslationVi: Mùa đông năm ngoái ở Vilnius, tôi có một vấn đề nhỏ với một chiếc răng, nên một đồng nghiệp đặt lịch hẹn cho tôi tại một phòng khám nha khoa trên phố Pylimo. Tôi đến sớm hai mươi phút vì tôi lo và không thể ngồi yên trong căn hộ. Phòng chờ nhỏ và ấm, với ba chiếc ghế gỗ, một chiếc bàn thấp phủ tạp chí cũ mà tôi không thể đọc, và một chiếc đồng hồ tròn lớn phía trên cửa. Trong khi tôi đang chờ, một cô bé trên ghế đối diện cứ quan sát tôi bằng khóe mắt. Mẹ cô bé đang đọc báo và giả vờ không để ý. Sau khoảng mười phút, cô bé đi ngang qua phòng, đặt một viên kẹo xanh nhỏ lên đầu gối tôi, và chạy về với mẹ. Tôi cố cảm ơn cô bé bằng tiếng Lithuania, nhưng tôi chỉ biết hai từ. Cô lễ tân ngẩng lên từ máy tính, mỉm cười, và dịch giúp tôi bằng tiếng Anh nhẹ nhàng. Khi nha sĩ cuối cùng gọi tên tôi, cuộc chờ đã cảm thấy ngắn hơn nhiều so với tôi tưởng. Tôi rời đi với một chiếc răng sạch sẽ được trám, một vỏ kẹo xanh trong túi, và một sự tôn trọng nhỏ mới với sự kiên nhẫn của trẻ con trong phòng chờ.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple opening.
options: Last winter in Vilnius, I had a small problem with one tooth.
Last winter in Vilnius, I have a small problem with one tooth.
Last winter in Vilnius, I am having a small problem with one tooth.
Last winter in Vilnius, I had had a small problem with one tooth.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'had' for a finished past situation with a definite time.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence about being early.
options: I arrived twenty minutes early because I was nervous.
I arrived twenty minutes earlier because I was nervous.
I arrived twenty minutes early because I am nervous.
I am arrived twenty minutes early because I was nervous.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Early' here is an adverb of time; past simple 'was' in the reason clause.

options: worried or slightly afraid of what will happen
very tired
very angry
very hungry

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct 'while' + past continuous sentence.
options: While I was waiting, a little girl kept watching me out of the corner of her eye.
While I waited, a little girl kept watching me out of the corner of her eye.
Both A and B are acceptable.
While I am waiting, a little girl kept watching me.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both past continuous and past simple can occur with 'while' — past continuous is more vivid; past simple is also grammatically fine.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence with 'pretend + to + infinitive'.
options: Her mother was reading a newspaper and pretending not to notice.
Her mother was reading a newspaper and pretending no to notice.
Her mother was reading a newspaper and pretending to not noticed.
Her mother was reading a newspaper and pretending no notice.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Pretend + (not) + to + base verb' is the standard pattern.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'out of the corner of her eye' mean?
options: looking at something secretly, without turning the head
with both eyes closed
with very bright eyes
while crying
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Out of the corner of one's eye' = peripheral, secretive glance.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the natural past simple sentence.
options: She walked across the room, put a sweet on my knee, and ran back to her mother.
She walk across the room, put a sweet on my knee, and ran back to her mother.
She walked across the room, putted a sweet on my knee, and ran back to her mother.
She was walking across the room, was putting a sweet on my knee, and was running back to her mother.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple chain ('walked, put, ran') — 'put' has the same past form.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence with 'try + to + infinitive'.
options: I tried to thank her in Lithuanian.
I tried thank her in Lithuanian.
I tried thanking her in Lithuanian.
I tried for thank her in Lithuanian.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Try + to + base verb' = make an attempt.

options: to express something said in one language using another language
to repeat the same word twice
to write in capital letters
to speak more loudly

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past perfect comparative sentence.
options: The wait had felt much shorter than I expected.
The wait have felt much shorter than I expected.
The wait was felt much shorter than I expected.
The wait had feel much shorter than I expected.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had felt' for retrospective evaluation; comparative 'shorter'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural closing sentence.
options: I left with a clean filling, a green sweet wrapper in my pocket, and a small new respect for the patience of children.
I left with a clean filling, a green sweet wrapper in my pocket, and a small new respect for the patience for children.
I left with a clean filling, a green sweet wrapper on my pocket, and a small new respect for the patience of children.
I left with a clean filling, a green sweet wrapper in my pocket, and a small new respect for the patient of children.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do strangers in waiting rooms often act kinder than strangers anywhere else in a city?
